_comment: This is a comprehensive reference configuration file for the signal generator. All components are in a single 'components' list. The application will correctly place them in the UI.

components._comment: Sine: A standard sine wave. Parameters: amplitude, frequency (Hz), phase (degrees).
Sine (Varying Freq): A sine wave that abruptly changes frequency at a specified time. Parameters: amplitude, start_freq (Hz), end_freq (Hz), change_time (s).
Cosine: A standard cosine wave, which is a sine wave with a 90-degree phase shift. Parameters: amplitude, frequency (Hz), phase (degrees).
Square: A square wave. Parameters: amplitude, frequency (Hz), phase (degrees), and duty_cycle (0.0 to 1.0, where 0.5 is a perfect square).
Chirp: A signal with a linearly increasing frequency over its duration. Parameters: amplitude, start_freq (Hz), end_freq (Hz).
Gaussian Noise: Adds random noise from a normal distribution. std_dev controls power. The seed makes the noise pattern reproducible. Use the 'Regenerate' button for a new pattern.
Amplitude Jump: An instantaneous DC shift in the signal from a certain point onwards. Parameters: jump_size (can be positive or negative), jump_time (s).
Impulse Noise: A single, sharp spike added to the signal at a specific time. Parameters: amplitude, impulse_time (s).
Outlier: Replaces a single data point with an extreme, absolute value. Parameters: value, outlier_time (s).
Bias: Adds a constant DC offset to the entire signal. Parameter: offset.
Drift: Adds a linearly increasing or decreasing trend to the signal. Parameter: slope (in units per second).
Signal Dropout: Forces the signal to be exactly zero for a specified duration. Parameters: start_time (s), duration (s).
Saturation: Clips the signal's amplitude if it exceeds a certain level. Parameter: threshold (clips the signal at +/- threshold).
Time Delay: Shifts the entire signal forward in time, padding the beginning with zeros. Parameter: delay (s).
